content:
DNA Funded Rules Overview

DNA Funded is a broker-backed proprietary trading firm established in 2024 and headquartered in Melbourne, Australia. Unlike many prop firms that operate as independent funding platforms, DNA Funded is directly backed by DNA Markets, an ASIC-regulated broker—a significant differentiator in an industry often criticized for opacity and regulatory concerns.

The firm operates on an evaluation challenge model. Rather than depositing your own capital, traders pay a challenge fee to access a demo or simulated trading account where they must meet specific profit targets and adhere to risk parameters. Upon successfully passing the evaluation, traders gain access to a funded live account where they can trade real markets and split profits with the firm. DNA Funded offers four distinct challenge pathways: 1-Phase, 2-Phase, Rapid (10-Day), and Instant Funding—each designed for different trader profiles and risk tolerances.

Account Types and Pricing

DNA Funded offers four challenge types with transparent, tiered pricing:

Challenge Type Monthly Fee Structure Best For
1 Phase (Single Helix) $49/month Single evaluation phase; no time limits Traders who prefer a single, unhurried evaluation
2 Phase (Double Helix) $49/month Two sequential evaluation phases; no time limits Traders wanting to prove consistency across two phases
Rapid (10-Day) $99/month Compressed evaluation in 10 trading days Experienced traders seeking faster funding decisions
Instant Funding $199/month Immediate funded account; no evaluation phase Established traders who want to skip evaluation entirely

Important Note: All challenge fees are refunded upon your first payout from the funded account, making the initial investment recoverable once you begin generating profit.

Profit Targets

DNA Funded requires traders to achieve specific profit targets during the evaluation phase. However, detailed profit target percentages for each challenge type are not fully disclosed in public documentation. Traders should expect:

  • 1-Phase and 2-Phase challenges: Typically require a consistent profit target (specific percentage varies by account size and is available upon sign-up)
  • Rapid (10-Day) challenge: Compressed profit targets designed to be achievable within a 10-trading-day window
  • Instant Funding: No profit target requirement—you receive funded capital immediately

Traders should contact DNA Funded directly or review their platform after account creation to see exact profit requirements for their chosen challenge tier. Profit targets are typically calculated as a percentage return on the demo or simulated account balance provided during evaluation.

Drawdown Rules

Drawdown management is critical in any prop firm evaluation. DNA Funded enforces the following risk controls:

  • Daily Drawdown Limit: A maximum daily loss limit applies, though the specific percentage is not publicly documented. Traders must adhere to this to avoid immediate account termination.
  • Maximum Drawdown (Trailing): DNA Funded uses a trailing drawdown model, meaning losses are measured from the highest equity point reached during trading. This is more forgiving than a static drawdown model, which measures losses from the starting account balance.
  • Calculation Method: Drawdown is calculated in real-time as trades are executed. The system monitors equity fluctuations and halts trading once the daily or maximum drawdown limit is breached.
  • Reset Conditions: Daily drawdown limits typically reset at the end of each trading day (UTC or broker server time). Maximum trailing drawdown does not reset and remains your highest equity benchmark until surpassed by new account growth.

Recommendation: Verify exact drawdown percentages by contacting DNA Funded's support or during the initial account setup, as these are critical to your evaluation strategy.

Trading Restrictions

To maintain fair evaluation conditions and manage systemic risk, DNA Funded enforces several trading restrictions:

  • Time Limits: 1-Phase and 2-Phase challenges have no time limits, allowing traders to evaluate at their own pace. The Rapid challenge operates within a 10-trading-day window. Instant Funding has no evaluation period.
  • Restricted Instruments:
    • Futures and options trading are not yet available on DNA Funded platforms
    • Cryptocurrency trading is excluded from the Rapid challenge but may be permitted in 1-Phase and 2-Phase challenges
  • Lot Size and Leverage Limits: Standard risk management parameters apply; specific lot size caps and maximum leverage are configured per account and should be verified during setup.
  • Weekend Holding: Holding positions over weekends is generally permitted on forex and CFD products; crypto holdings over weekends may have additional restrictions on the Rapid challenge.
  • News Trading: Specific restrictions during high-impact news events are not explicitly documented. Best practice is to assume tight trading windows around major economic data releases and central bank announcements.
  • Expert Advisors (EAs) and Automated Trading: DNA Funded supports algorithmic trading and automated systems. Verify that your EA complies with any speed or frequency restrictions on the TradeLocker platform.
  • Copy Trading: Copy trading restrictions are not explicitly mentioned in available documentation; contact support to confirm whether copying other traders' signals is permitted during evaluation.

Profit Split and Payouts

DNA Funded's profit-sharing structure is one of the most attractive in the industry:

  • Profit Split: Traders retain 80% of all profits generated on funded accounts, with DNA Funded taking 20%. This is significantly more generous than many competitors and reflects the firm's broker-backed model.
  • Withdrawal Frequency: Payouts are typically processed on a scheduled basis (weekly or bi-weekly; verify exact frequency with the platform).
  • Minimum Withdrawal Amount: A minimum withdrawal threshold may apply; specific amount varies by region and account status.
  • Processing Time: Withdrawals are typically processed within 5–10 business days, with transfers handled through banking or cryptocurrency methods depending on your account settings.
  • Challenge Fee Refund: Your initial $49–$199 challenge fee is refunded automatically upon your first payout, meaning your first withdrawal will include the fee reimbursement.

The 80% profit split is particularly competitive. For comparison, many prop firms offer 50–70% splits, so DNA Funded's terms are advantageous for serious traders looking to maximize their earnings.

Scaling Plan

Information on DNA Funded's scaling plan—how account sizes increase over time—is not extensively documented in public materials. However, typical prop firm scaling models work as follows:

  • Initial Account Size: Traders begin with a demo or live funded account of a set balance (e.g., $10,000–$50,000).
  • Performance Milestones: Accounts typically scale after reaching profit milestones or trading consistency targets.
  • Scaling Timeline: Increases may occur monthly or quarterly, depending on performance metrics.
  • Requirements: Maintaining compliance with drawdown rules and demonstrating profitable trading are standard prerequisites for scaling.

Action Item: Contact DNA Funded directly for specific account scaling details, as this information is usually provided during onboarding or available in the trader dashboard.

DNA Funded Rules vs Competitors

To contextualize DNA Funded's rules, here's how they compare to leading competitors:

Rule Category DNA Funded Competitor A Competitor B
Profit Split 80% trader 70% trader 60% trader
Entry Cost $49/month $99/month $299 one-time
Drawdown Model Trailing (Lenient) Static (Strict) Trailing (Lenient)
Time Limit (1-Phase) None (Lenient) 30 days (Strict) 60 days (Moderate)
Broker Regulation ASIC-backed (Strong) FCA-regulated Unregulated (Weak)
Instant Funding Available Yes ($199/mo) No Yes ($499/mo)

Key Advantages:

  • DNA Funded's 80% profit split is among the best in the industry, making it highly attractive for profitable traders.
  • The no time limit on 1-Phase and 2-Phase challenges removes evaluation pressure and is lenient compared to competitors with fixed deadlines.
  • ASIC regulation backing provides stronger credibility and trader protection than unregulated or lightly regulated alternatives.
  • The trailing drawdown model is more forgiving than static models, giving traders room to recover from setbacks.

Potential Disadvantages:

  • Monthly fees ($49–$199) for some challenges may accumulate if you take multiple attempts, whereas some competitors offer one-time fees.
  • As a 2024-founded firm, DNA Funded has limited historical data and community track record compared to established competitors with years of operation.

Red Flags and Fine Print

While DNA Funded presents many attractive features, traders should be aware of the following considerations:

  • Young Company (Founded 2024): The firm is relatively new, with limited long-term community feedback and regulatory history. Exercise caution and start with small investment amounts until the platform proves itself over time.
  • Futures and Options Unavailable: If you trade derivatives or complex instruments, you're currently limited to forex, indices, commodities, stocks, and CFD products. Futures and options may be added later, but no timeline is provided.
  • Cryptocurrency Restrictions on Rapid Challenge: Crypto is excluded from the 10-day Rapid challenge but apparently available on other tiers. Verify crypto trading parameters before committing if crypto is core to your strategy.
  • Affiliate Commission Rates Undisclosed: If you plan to refer traders, affiliate commission rates are not publicly published. This should be clarified with the support team before promoting the platform.
  • Challenge Fee Refund Timing: While the challenge fee is refunded on your first payout, the refund process and exact timing should be confirmed. Verify that refunds are immediate or if there's a delay.
  • Profit Target Percentages Not Clearly Published: Exact profit targets are not detailed in marketing materials. Confirm these before opening an account so you understand your evaluation benchmark.
  • Account Size and Leverage Specifics: Maximum leverage, starting account sizes, and lot size limits should be verified, as these directly impact your risk management.
  • Monthly Subscription Model: Unlike one-time challenge fees, the monthly billing means ongoing costs even during periods when you're not actively trading. Ensure you understand renewal and cancellation policies.

Best Practice: Before funding any account, thoroughly read DNA Funded's full terms of service, risk disclosure, and trader agreement. Contact their support team with any ambiguities, and consider starting with the 1-Phase challenge at the lowest tier ($49) to evaluate the platform and support quality.
